description:
You are the on-call artifact manager. Three on-premise binary repositories are intermittently unreachable and the build-farm team has asked you for a small, machine-readable network-diagnostic bundle that they can ingest into their monitoring backend.

Your job (from inside the terminal, as the user “user”) is to:

1. Discover the list of repositories  
   • A plain-text file already exists at  
     /home/user/repositories/targets.list  
     Every non-blank, non-comment line has the format  

       <repo-name><single-space><ipv4-address>  

     e.g.  
       repo-alpha 127.0.0.1  
       repo-beta 127.0.0.1  

2. For each repository in that list perform, **in parallel**, the following two diagnostics:  
   a. `ping` exactly 4 echo requests (`-c 4`) to the target IP.  
   b. `traceroute` with a maximum of 6 hops (`-m 6`) and numeric output (`-n`).

3. Parse the two command outputs and create one consolidated JSON-Lines log file at  
   /home/user/artifacts/net_diagnostics_aggregated.log  
   This file **must** contain one line per repository, strictly in the order they appear in targets.list, with the following keys and value types:

   • host          (string)  – the literal repository name from the first column  
   • ip            (string)  – the IPv4 address from the second column  
   • avg_ping_ms   (number)  – the average RTT reported by ping, rounded to one decimal place  
   • hop_count     (integer) – the total number of hops actually shown in the traceroute run  
   • timestamp     (string)  – the ISO-8601 UTC time **at which you write the JSON object**  
   
   Example of two valid JSON-Lines entries (do **not** copy these exact numbers—they are only illustrative):

   {"host":"repo-alpha","ip":"127.0.0.1","avg_ping_ms":0.042,"hop_count":1,"timestamp":"2023-11-25T16:03:12Z"}  
   {"host":"repo-beta","ip":"127.0.0.1","avg_ping_ms":0.039,"hop_count":1,"timestamp":"2023-11-25T16:03:12Z"}

   Formatting requirements that the automated grader will enforce:
   • UTF-8 encoded, Unix newlines (`\n`).  
   • Exactly one JSON object per line, no leading/trailing spaces outside the JSON text, no trailing comma inside the object, and no blank lines anywhere.  
   • The file must contain **exactly** the same number of lines as there are usable (non-comment, non-blank) lines in targets.list.  
   • Keys must appear in the order shown above.

4. Produce a short human-readable Markdown file summarising what you did.  
   Location: /home/user/artifacts/README.md  
   Required headings and structure:  

   # Network Diagnostics Bundle yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM UTC  
   ## Targets Enumerated  
   (bullet list, one bullet per repo in the same order)  
   ## Tools & Versions  
   (bullet list with the exact command names you used, e.g. “ping (iputils)”, “traceroute (net-tools)”)  
   ## How To Reproduce  
   (an ordered list of steps, each on its own line)  

No other files or directories should be created or modified. All diagnostics have to be launched in parallel (for example with background jobs or GNU parallel) so that the total runtime stays well under the sequential sum of the individual pings and traceroutes.

The grading script will:

• Verify that /home/user/artifacts/net_diagnostics_aggregated.log exists and is non-empty.  
• Parse each JSON line and check that all required keys are present, in order, with the correct data types.  
• Ensure the “host” values exactly match the repository names appearing in /home/user/repositories/targets.list and appear in the same order.  
• Check that the hop_count field is a positive integer ≤ 6.  
• Confirm that the Markdown summary (/home/user/artifacts/README.md) contains the required section headings literally as specified above.

Remember: do not hard-code any values from the example; always derive your output from the actual content of /home/user/repositories/targets.list that is present on the system you are running on.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/repositories already exists (mode 755, owner user:user).  
2. File /home/user/repositories/targets.list (mode 644) contains exactly three lines (final line terminated with a newline):  
   repo-alpha 127.0.0.1  
   repo-beta 127.0.0.1  
   repo-gamma 127.0.0.1  

3. Directory /home/user/artifacts exists (mode 755, owner user:user) and is empty.

Verification criteria:

A. /home/user/artifacts/net_diagnostics_aggregated.log must exist, be readable, and contain exactly 3 newline-separated JSON objects, one per repository, in the order alpha → beta → gamma.  
B. Each object’s keys must appear exactly in this sequence: host, ip, avg_ping_ms, hop_count, timestamp.  
C. host values must be “repo-alpha”, “repo-beta”, “repo-gamma”.  
D. ip values must be “127.0.0.1”.  
E. avg_ping_ms is parsed as a JSON number >= 0.0 (float).  
F. hop_count is parsed as a JSON integer >= 1 and ≤ 6.  
G. timestamp matches the regex: ^20\d\d-[0-1]\d-[0-3]\dT[0-2]\d:[0-5]\d:[0-5]\dZ$  
H. /home/user/artifacts/README.md must exist and include the exact literal headings:  
   “# Network Diagnostics Bundle” (followed by a space and something)  
   “## Targets Enumerated”  
   “## Tools & Versions”  
   “## How To Reproduce”

No other files should be present in /home/user/artifacts/.
